steps.title: Find the constant b using the initial height
steps.content:
At time $t = 0$, the object is dropped from a height of 3,364 feet. Substituting into the function:
$$h(0) = -16(0)^2 + b = 3,364$$
Therefore $b = 3,364$, and the complete function is:
$$h(t) = -16t^2 + 3,364$$

steps.title: Solve for when the object hits the ground
steps.content:
The object hits the ground when $h(t) = 0$. Set up the equation:
$$-16t^2 + 3,364 = 0$$
Divide both sides by $-16$:
$$t^2 = \frac{3,364}{16} = 210.25$$
Take the positive square root (since time is positive):
$$t = \sqrt{210.25} = 14.50 \text{ seconds}$$
This matches choice B — B is correct.
